terms.definition: The process of making cloth by crossing two sets of threads (warp and weft) over and under each other on a loom.
terms.exampleUsage: Weaving is a traditional craft practised by many families in Pochampalli.

terms.definition: A frame or machine used for weaving cloth. The weaver stretches threads on it and interlaces them to make fabric.
terms.exampleUsage: The weaver sat at the loom from early morning to make a beautiful saree.

terms.definition: A loom that is operated by hand and foot power, without electricity. It produces unique, handmade cloth.
terms.exampleUsage: Pochampalli weavers use handlooms to create their famous Ikat sarees.

terms.definition: A special dyeing technique where threads are tied and dyed with colours before they are woven, creating beautiful blurred patterns.
terms.exampleUsage: The Ikat technique gives Pochampalli sarees their distinctive look.

terms.definition: The set of threads that run lengthwise (up and down) on a loom. They are stretched tightly before weaving begins.
terms.exampleUsage: The weaver carefully set up the warp threads on the loom before starting.

terms.definition: The set of threads that are passed horizontally (side to side) through the warp threads to create cloth.
terms.exampleUsage: The shuttle carries the weft thread across the warp on the loom.

terms.definition: A tool used in weaving to carry the weft thread back and forth across the warp threads on a loom.
terms.exampleUsage: The weaver threw the shuttle quickly from one side to the other.

terms.definition: A skilled worker who makes things by hand using traditional methods, such as a weaver, potter, or carpenter.
terms.exampleUsage: The artisans of Pochampalli are known for their exceptional weaving skills.

terms.definition: A smooth, shiny fabric made from the threads produced by silkworms. It is used to make expensive and beautiful sarees.
terms.exampleUsage: Pochampalli silk sarees are prized for their beautiful patterns and quality.

terms.definition: A movement in India that encouraged people to use goods made in India rather than foreign products, supporting local artisans and workers.
terms.exampleUsage: The Swadeshi Movement inspired Indians to buy handloom cloth instead of British machine-made fabric.
